title: PHP PDO Prepared Statements
codeExample:
setAttribute(PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE, PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION); // Bulk insert with prepared statement $sql = "INSERT INTO users (name, email, age) VALUES (:name, :email, :age)"; $stmt = $pdo->prepare($sql); $users = [ ["name" => "Alice", "email" => "alice@test.com", "age" => 25], ["name" => "Bob", "email" => "bob@test.com", "age" => 30], ["name" => "Charlie", "email" => "charlie@test.com", "age" => 35] ]; foreach ($users as $user) { $stmt->execute($user); echo "Inserted: " . $pdo->lastInsertId() . "
"; } // Select with prepared statement $searchStmt = $pdo->prepare( "SELECT * FROM users WHERE age BETWEEN :min AND :max ORDER BY age" ); $searchStmt->execute(["min" => 20, "max" => 35]); echo "

Users aged 20-35:

"; foreach ($searchStmt->fetchAll() as $user) { echo "{$user['name']} ({$user['age']}) - {$user['email']}
"; } } catch (PDOException $e) { echo "Database error: " . $e->getMessage(); } ?>

quiz.question: What does a prepared statement protect against?
Which method executes a prepared statement in PDO?
